task: I need to put together a simple handoff note for a customer issue that’s been bouncing around a bit, and I want it to be easy for the next person to follow. What’s the cleanest way to lay out the status, what’s already been checked, and what still needs attention without making it too wordy?

turns.agent_response:
Use a short, fixed structure every time so the next person can scan it fast.

### Simple handoff note format

**1) Issue / customer ask**  
- What the customer wants or what went wrong, in one sentence

**2) Current status**  
- Where it stands right now
- What’s still open

**3) Already checked**  
- Facts only
- What was verified, by whom, and when if needed

**4) Still needs attention**  
- What hasn’t been resolved yet
- Who owns it, or who needs to do the next step

**5) Next step / deadline**  
- What should happen next
- When it’s due or when the next update is expected

---

### Working template

**Issue:**  
Customer reports [problem].

**Status:**  
Current state is [status]. Issue is [open / partially resolved / waiting on X].

**Already checked:**  
- [record / note / action checked]
- [result]
- [result]

**Still needs attention:**  
- [open question or missing piece]
- [owner]

**Next step:**  
[What happens next] by [time/date].

---

### Example

**Issue:**  
Customer says order arrived incomplete.

**Status:**  
We confirmed the order was delivered. Missing items are still unverified.

**Already checked:**  
- Order record matches the shipment
- Delivery was completed at 2:14 PM
- Customer provided photos of the box

**Still needs attention:**  
- Need warehouse check on pack-out
- Need confirmation whether items were packed or omitted

**Next step:**  
Warehouse to review packing records and update by end of day.

If you want, I can also turn this into a **one-paragraph version** or a **fillable note you can copy/paste**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically the right shape. Clean and usable.

I’d tighten it a little more for real handoff use though — keep it more factual, less “template-y.” I’d want:

- **Customer / issue**
- **Current status**
- **What’s been checked**
- **What still needs action**
- **Owner / next update**

And under each one, just 1–3 bullets. No long sentences unless you need one for context.

If you want it to be easy for the next person, the key is this: **separate what is confirmed from what is still pending**. That’s what saves the back-and-forth later. I’ve had to clean up a lot of messy handoffs where people mixed guesses in with verified info, and that just causes more calls.

A simple version would look like:

**Customer / issue:**
- Customer reports [problem]

**Current status:**
- [Open / partially resolved / waiting on X]

**Checked already:**
- [Record reviewed]
- [Customer contact / warehouse / dispatch / billing checked]
- [Confirmed facts]

**Still needs action:**
- [Open item]
- [Who needs to handle it]

**Owner / next update:**
- [Name or team]
- [Due time / date]

If you want, I can make it even tighter — like a version that fits in 5 lines max for internal notes.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — keep it factual, short, and in the same order every time.

### Clean handoff note format

**Customer / issue**
- Who it is and what the problem is, in one line

**Current status**
- Where it stands right now
- Open / waiting / partially resolved

**Checked already**
- Facts that have been verified
- Records, contacts, photos, tracking, notes, etc.

**Still needs action**
- What is still unresolved
- Who needs to handle it next

**Owner / next update**
- Who owns the next step
- When the next update is due

### Simple rule
Keep **confirmed facts** separate from **open items**.  
If something is still a guess, don’t put it in the checked section.

### Example

**Customer / issue**
- Customer says order arrived short

**Current status**
- Delivery confirmed
- Missing items not yet verified

**Checked already**
- Order record reviewed
- Delivery time confirmed
- Customer photos received

**Still needs action**
- Warehouse to check pack-out
- Need confirmation on whether items were omitted

**Owner / next update**
- Warehouse team
- Update due by end of day

If you want, I can turn this into a **5-line ultra-short version** for internal handoffs.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is better.

I’d still cut the wording a bit more so it reads like an actual handoff note, not instructions. Real simple, more like:

**Customer / issue**  
- [Name] — [problem]

**Current status**  
- [open / waiting / partially resolved]
- [one factual status line]

**Checked already**  
- [record / contact / photo / tracking reviewed]
- [confirmed fact]
- [confirmed fact]

**Still needs action**  
- [open item]
- [next person or team]

**Owner / next update**  
- [owner]
- [due date/time]

That keeps it clean and avoids the extra explanation. The big thing is still the same — don’t mix verified stuff with what someone thinks might be true.

If you want, I’d actually want the **5-line ultra-short version** too. That’s the one I’d use most of the time.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the **5-line ultra-short version** in the same style:

**Customer / issue**  
- [Name] — [problem]

**Current status**  
- [open / waiting / partially resolved]  
- [one factual status line]

**Checked already**  
- [record / contact / photo / tracking reviewed]  
- [confirmed fact]  
- [confirmed fact]

**Still needs action**  
- [open item]  
- [next person or team]

**Owner / next update**  
- [owner]  
- [due date/time]

### Quick rule
Keep **checked already** to facts only.  
If it’s not confirmed, leave it in **still needs action**.

If you want, I can also make this into a **copy/paste blank note** with placeholders you can reuse.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the one. Clean, short, and actually usable.

The only thing I’d keep in mind is exactly what you said — facts only in **Checked already**. No guessing, no soft filler. That part matters.

This version is good enough to use as-is.
